source: internal hunt (not shipped as repo files); every number in this ledger was independently re-derived from experimental/data/certificates/l1-e3-law/l1_e3_law_refutation.json (the six witnesses) or re-run from scratch (the toy sweep), not merely copied
target: the post-refutation open ceiling E_3 <= ell + C' (experimental/notes/l1/l1_e3_law_refuted.md Sec 0 item 6; experimental/notes/l1/l1_e3_dim_syz_crux_refuted.md Sec 6, PR #365, concurrent)
verdict: C' observed as +2 (W3, ell=17, p=137), NOT proved extremal. Three exact pieces of structure (identity, Lemma-R sub-ceiling, q-plane concurrency reformulation) narrate but do not close the gap. 0 configs with excess>=3 found across 425 swept plants (ell in {17,19}).

excess_identity.formula: excess := E_3 - ell = T - 4 - capslack, where capslack := ell - (mu_1+mu_2) >= 0 (the PROVED pairwise cap)
excess_identity.consequence: excess >= 3  iff  a realizable config carries T >= 7+capslack (T >= 7 at a cap-tight top pair, capslack=0)
excess_identity.canonical_shape_consequence: for [ell-3,3^k]: T=k-1, capslack=0, excess=k-5; excess=+3 needs exactly k>=8 concurrent size-3 fibers

identity_table.note: the one non-cap-tight witness (capslack=1); sharpest sanity check of the identity

lemma_r_subceiling.lemma_r_source: experimental/notes/l1/l1_e3_charsum_paircap.md, Proposition (pair-cap), PROVED: sum_b mu_b(mu_b-1) <= (ell-1)(ell-2) (consistent with the same-named citations in l1_prime_ell_onset.md and l1_prime_ell_pv_refutation.md)
lemma_r_subceiling.shape: [ell-3, 3^k]: one fiber of size ell-3, k fibers of size 3 (forced by the pairwise cap once mu_1=ell-3 is planted, since mu_2 <= ell-mu_1 = 3, and min-mu>=3 normalization removes anything smaller)

table.status: Lemma R alone proves excess<=0 here (a fortiori <=2)
Lemma R alone proves excess<=2 here (tight to the stated consequence)
realizability-limited: Lemma R permits +4, observed max is +2 (W3) -- gap of 2

lemma_r_subceiling.consequence: ell<=13 => excess<=2 PROVED for this shape (the only two odd primes <=13 with ell-3>=3); NOT proved at ell=17 (permits +4)

qplane_spotcheck_w3.setup: F0 = the known size-14 planted fiber of W3 (ell=17,p=137); g0 = monic degree-14 locator vanishing on F0; Gamma = g0*q + lambda0 forces q of degree exactly 2 (the P^2(F_p) 'q-plane')
qplane_spotcheck_w3.reconstruction_check: g0*q + lambda0 reproduces the shipped W3 gamma exactly: PASS
qplane_spotcheck_w3.concurrency_verdict: all 7 tail 3-fibers, solved INDEPENDENTLY of each other and without using the global Gamma, recover the identical q-plane point (1,0,58) -- concurrency demonstrated by direct computation, not asserted

coverage_ledger.description: 425 plants swept total; P^2(F_p) sweep exhaustive per plant (no member skipped); dropset (plant) selection is a deterministic sample, not exhaustive, except where the possible-dropset count is small enough to state exactly

rows.note: rediscovers the already-shipped W3 witness's exact invariants from the hunt's own combinatorial generation; validates the method against a known answer
under-covered: slowest prime here (|P^2(F_409)|=167691 members/plant), only 1 of 18 requested plants completed

rows.correction_1: source hunt labeled this row '130/130 (exhaustive triples)'; C(19,3)=969, so 130 is a ~13.4%-coverage deterministic sample of dropsets, NOT exhaustive over them -- corrected. The per-plant P^2 sweep IS exhaustive (52671 points checked), and all 130 requested plants completed (no time-cutoff).
rows.correction_2: max excess at this row is +0 (E_3=19=ell exactly), not +1 -- confirmed both by the source hunt's own JSON and by an independent from-scratch resweep of the reported best dropset (4.14s, reproducing the spectrum exactly).

undercovered: ell in {23,29}: not run at all
coverage_ledger.undercovered_justification: the ell=17 n=14/18 trend (concurrency count FALLING to 5 as n grows from 8) already refutes the hope that larger n helps; Lemma R permits strictly MORE excess at ell in {23,29} (+8,+12) than at ell=17 (+4), so by the q-plane mechanism (Sec 3 of the note) these are expected to be at least as capped, not less -- left unrun by budget, not by proof

corrections_from_source_hunt.correction: C(19,3)=969; 130 is a deterministic spread sample (~13.4% of possible dropsets), not exhaustive over the dropset space. The per-plant P^2(F_229) sweep (52671 points) IS exhaustive, and all 130 requested plants completed with no time-cutoff.
corrections_from_source_hunt.impact: none on the qualitative finding (max excess=0, no win); corrects only the word 'exhaustive' as applied to dropset coverage

corrections_from_source_hunt.claim: max excess at the (ell=19,p=229,n=12) row is +1
corrections_from_source_hunt.correction: the correct value is +0 (E_3=19=ell exactly), confirmed both by the source hunt's own JSON (max_excess: 0) and by an independent from-scratch resweep of the reported best dropset (4.14s, spectrum [16,3,3,3,3,3,2,2,2,2,2,2] reproduced exactly).
corrections_from_source_hunt.impact: this row contributes no T>=5 excess evidence either way; it is a correctly-null result for the excess>=3 hunt, not a T=5-adjacent near miss

concurrent_open_prs.relationship: concurrent, not integrated, not depended on; independently concludes m*(19) stays {8,9}, consistent with this note's own no-m=8-crossing observation

concurrent, not integrated, not depended on; independently names the identical bounded-excess target E_3<=ell+C' and the same (W,lambda)-Veronese transversality as the open core; this ledger supplies one shape's worth of concrete structure toward that same target

non_claims: C'<=2 is NOT proved -- it is a localized, three-times-corroborated observation, not a theorem. The open core (no realizable T>=7 tail) is unchanged and open. ell in {23,29} not run. The [ell-4,4,3^k] shape was swept but not analyzed geometrically here.
